data.title: Solovis secures support from Insight Partners and ST6 for growth phase. Leading multi-asset-class platform for institutional investors.
data.description: Solovis, a prominent multi-asset-class portfolio management and analytics platform for institutional investors, has reinforced its reputation as a reliable ally for asset owners in navigating intricate global markets. The company, based in New York, disclosed on Oct. 16, 2025, a significant milestone as it finalized its latest venture.

data.title: XRP whale selling stirs volatility. Consider CLS Mining, earning 3,000 XRP/day. XRP surges to $2.50 in a day, with optimistic outlook. Institutional confidence high with REX-Osprey XRP ETF at $90M.
data.description: XRP surged to $2.50 in just one day, accompanied by high trading activity, leading experts to express optimism about its future peak levels. The institutional ownership of XRP remains steady, notably the REX-Osprey XRP ETF overseeing assets exceeding $90 million, showcasing firm belief in its long-term potential.

data.title: CSX Corporation to release Q3 earnings on Oct 16. Analysts predict earnings at 42 cents/share, revenue at $3.57 billion. CEO appointment mentioned. Stock shares rose 0.3% to close at $36.24 on Wednesday.
data.description: CSX Corporation (NASDAQ: CSX) is scheduled to release its third-quarter earnings on Thursday, Oct. 16 after the market closes. Analysts are anticipating quarterly earnings of 42 cents per share, a decrease from 46 cents per share in the same period last year. The consensus forecast for CSX's quarterly revenue stands at $3.57 billion, down from $3.62 billion in the previous year. On Sept. 29, Steve Angel was named as the new President and CEO of CSX. The company's shares saw a 0.3% increase, closing at $36.24 on Wednesday. For the latest analyst ratings, readers can visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga, where information can be filtered by stock ticker, company name, analyst firm, rating change, and other criteria.

data.title: Interactive Brokers likely to report higher Q3 earnings; analysts expect 54 cents per share, $1.52B revenue, up from $1.33B y-o-y. Ask IBKR tool launched. Stock rose 0.6% to $69.77.
data.description: Interactive Brokers Group, Inc. will unveil its third-quarter earnings after market close on Thursday, October 16. Analysts project earnings of 54 cents per share, an increase from last year's 44 cents per share. Revenue for the quarter is expected to reach $1.52 billion, up from $1.33 billion in the previous year. The company recently introduced Ask IBKR, an AI-driven tool for immediate portfolio insights. On October 15, the company's stock closed at $69.77, marking a 0.6% increase. Access to further analyst ratings can be found on the Analyst Stock Ratings page.

data.title: Abbott Laboratories reports Q3 sales of $11.37 billion, slightly missing consensus. Adjusted earnings at $1.30 in line with estimates. Fiscal 2025 guidance revised from $5.10-$5.20/share. CEO confident in company's consistent performance and product pipeline.
data.description: Abbott Laboratories (NYSE: ABT) announced its third-quarter sales of $11.37 billion, slightly below the expected $11.40 billion. The U.S. MedTech giant also reported adjusted earnings of $1.30 per share in line with expectations. Chairman and CEO Robert B. Ford emphasized the consistent performance and the strong product pipeline driving long-term value for shareholders. The company provided updated fiscal 2025 adjusted earnings guidance, narrowing it from $5.10-$5.20 per share. Abbott Laboratories continues to demonstrate its ability to deliver high-quality results and remains optimistic about its future prospects.

data.title: Natural Grocers offering discounted prices on 500+ items until Dec. 31, 2025. Savings include $1.99 eggs, $3.99 butters, $0.99 avocados for {N}power® Members.
data.description: Discounted items like free range eggs for $1.99, butters for $3.99, and avocados for $0.99 are now available for {N}power® Members at Natural Grocers® in LAKEWOOD, Colo. These super-powered savings were announced on Oct. 16, 2025. Natural Grocers® is recognized as the foremost family-operated retailer in the natural and organic sector in the United States. This exclusive offer comes just in time for the upcoming holiday season, offering significant discounts on essential food items.

data.title: Conceal secures $26M in Series B funding led by Two Bear Capital to boost product development and corporate strategies.
data.description: An injection of funds is set to accelerate product development speed, market strategies, and other vital corporate plans. The investment announcement came from Augusta, Georgia on October 16, 2025. Conceal, a leading provider of browser-native Security Service Edge (SSE) and proxy-less Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA) technology, is at the forefront of this initiative.

data.title: Axiado and Inventec unveil AI-driven platform security for NVIDIA Blackwell and Intel MGX Servers at OCP Summit 2025.
data.description: In a recent announcement at OCP Global Summit 2025 in San Jose, California, Axiado Corporation, a prominent provider of hardware-anchored platform security and system management solutions powered by AI, revealed a new partnership with Inventec. Inventec, renowned for its innovative server and storage solutions worldwide, will be collaborating with Axiado Corporation to enhance their offerings in this space. This strategic collaboration is set to bring cutting-edge technology to market and drive advancements in the industry.

data.title: Gold hits record high at $4,270/oz, silver at $53.90. Gold up 63% YTD. GDX rose 4%, 150% YTD. Bitcoin falls below $110,000.
data.description: Gold and silver reached unprecedented heights on Wall Street, with gold hitting $4,270 per ounce and silver climbing to $53.90. Gold rose 1.5% on Thursday, marking its 14th positive session out of 16 and increasing its year-to-date rally to 63%, the best since 1979. The VanEck Gold Miners ETF (GDX) surged 4% on Thursday and 12% for the week, with a remarkable year-to-date return of 150%, outperforming all other equity industries on Wall Street. In comparison, the Global X Artificial Intelligence & Technology ETF (AIQ) had a 30% year-to-date gain. This rally was supported by dovish comments on another rate cut from Federal Reserve Gov. Christopher Waller.

data.title: United Airlines Holdings, Inc. reported Q3 FY2025 earnings with a revenue increase to $15.2 billion, including 48 million customers flown. They expect margin expansion in 2026 with a focus on customer investments and efficiency improvements. Joining the call were CEO Scott Kirby, President Brett Hart, EVP Andrew Nisella, and CFO Mike Leskinen. Brand loyalty, technology investments, and cost efficiency are key strategies for United's growth. Revenue was up 2.6% with a 7.2% capacity increase, while consolidated TRASM was down 4.3%. International flights saw PRASM down 7.1% with a strong performance in premium cabins. United expects consolidated RASM to improve in Q4 and aims for the highest revenue quarter ever in 2025. They are investing in pilots and flight attendants, and technology enhancements for the customer experience. Operational resilience and cost management are highlighted, with a significant focus on cost efficiency through technology and strategic investments. The company remains committed to enhancing customer value and building brand loyalty.
data.description: United Airlines Holdings, Inc. released its Q3 2025 earnings report, citing a 2.6% increase in revenues to $15.2 billion and a 7.2% increase in capacity. The company reported a consolidated TRASM decrease of 4.3% and domestic PRASM down 3.3% in Q3. Premium cabins outperformed the main cabin. The company flew over 48 million customers in the quarter and had its lowest rate of cancellations in any Q3, with six of seven hubs ranking first or second for on-time departures. United expects its Q4 to be the best revenue quarter ever, with the highest absolute RASM of 2025. The company plans to hire over 2000 pilots and 3200 new flight attendants in 2026. United has been investing over $1 billion annually in customer product enhancements and anticipates spending another billion next year. The company emphasized investments in technology to drive efficiency, improve customer experience, and reduce costs. United's management remains focused on building brand loyalty and driving double-digit margins in the future.

data.title: Progressive's Q3 earnings missed estimates: $4.06/share vs $5.04/share. Quarterly sales were $20.849B vs $21.819B estimate. Shares down 2.2%.
data.description: Progressive Corporation (NYSE: PGR) announced disappointing third-quarter earnings, with earnings per share at $4.06, falling short of the expected $5.04 per share. Quarterly sales were reported at $20.849 billion, below the estimated $21.819 billion. Consequently, Progressive's stock dropped by 2.2% to $221.57. Following the earnings report, B of A Securities analyst Joshua Shanker maintained a Buy rating on Progressive and increased the price target. For more details, refer to the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: By 2033, the United States Digital Payment market is predicted to grow more than threefold to reach $9.29 trillion, with a CAGR of 13.11% from 2025-2033.
data.description: In Dublin, a comprehensive report titled "United States Digital Payment Market Report by Type, Offering, End Use, States and Company Analysis, 2025-2033" has been released and made available through ResearchAndMarkets.com. The United States Digital Payment Market is projected to experience significant expansion, with anticipated growth from US$ 3.06 trillion in 2024 to US$ 9.29 trillion by 2033. This growth reflects an impressive Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 13.11% expected during the period spanning from 2025 to 2033. The surge in digital payment adoption is driving this growth trend.

data.title: doxoBILLS offers free bill payments from any checking account across 10,000+ banks & credit unions, aiming to simplify bill management and reduce potential overdrafts.
data.description: In Seattle, a new solution is aiming to streamline the management of monthly bills for consumers. Keeping track of various biller websites, due dates, and payment accounts has traditionally been a time-consuming and disjointed process. The lack of real-time visibility often results in overdrafts, payment fees, and added stress. One of the key features of doxoBILLS All-in-One Bill Pay is the Real-Time Bank Balance with Linked Bank Account. By offering real-time access to bank balances, this feature aims to address these issues, helping users avoid financial pitfalls.

data.title: Coinbase introduces platform for small businesses with crypto payments, stablecoin yields, and accounting integration.
data.description: Coinbase introduced a new business platform tailored for small and midsize companies, providing access to crypto payments, stablecoin yields, and seamless accounting integrations. This move aims to assist firms in leveraging cryptocurrencies effectively.
